title: FINRA Case 25-01676 — Ramona Ivy v. Edward Jones
text:
Expungement Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-01676
Ramona Ivy
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Los Angeles, California
Edward D. Jones & Co., L.P.
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are appointed pursuant to Rule 13806 of
the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) to issue final, binding decisions. FINRA makes
available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by the SEC—but has no part in
deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Member
The expungement hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Ramona Ivy (“Claimant”): David Harrison, Esq., Bakhtiari & Harrison, P.C., Studio
City, California.
For Respondent Edward D. Jones & Co., L.P. (“Respondent”): Kevin H. Lewis, Esq., Edward D.
Jones & Co., L.P., St. Louis, Missouri.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: August 13, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: August 8, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Respondent on or about: October 7, 2025.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: October 7, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted a claim seeking expungement of customer dispute
information from registration records maintained by the Central Registration Depository (“CRD”).
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent took no position on Claimant’s expungement request.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01676
Award Page 2 of 4
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant (CRD Number 7083803) requested: Expungement of all
references to Occurrence Number 2394302 from the CRD registration records and any and all
other relief that the Panel deems appropriate.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested that Claimant should be required to bear all
fees and costs associated with this proceeding.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
On February 26, 2026, Claimant advised that the customer in Occurrence Number 2394302
(“Customer”) was served with a copy of the Statement of Claim, although not within 10 days of
filing the Statement of Claim, and provided proof of service.
On February 26, 2026, Claimant advised that the Customer was served with a copy of the
Statement of Answer, although not within 10 days of filing the Statement of Answer, and
provided proof of service.
On February 27, 2026, the authorized representative of the state securities regulator for California
submitted a written statement.
The Panel conducted a recorded hearing by videoconference on March 17, 2026, so the parties
could present oral argument and evidence on Claimant’s request for expungement. Claimant
appeared by videoconference at the expungement hearing. Respondent appeared at the
expungement hearing.
The Customer did not appear at the expungement hearing.
The authorized representative of the state securities regulator for California did not appear at the
expungement hearing. On September 2, 2025, the authorized representative of the state securities
regulator for California submitted a written statement with their intent to appear at the expungement
hearing. In their written statement submitted on February 27, 2026, the authorized representative
of the state securities regulator for California stated their intention not to appear at the
expungement hearing.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the expungement
hearing, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues submitted for
determination as follows:
1. Claimant’s request for expungement of Occurrence Number 2394302 from registration
records maintained by the CRD is denied with prejudice. The Panel’s decision was
unanimous.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01676
Award Page 3 of 4
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 2,000.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,600.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,005.00
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
One (1) pre-hearing session with the Panel @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Pre-Hearing Conference: November 18, 2025 1 session
One (1) hearing session on expungement request @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Hearing: March 17, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 3,450.00
The Panel has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01676
Award Page 4 of 4
ARBITRATION PANEL
Diane Ciccone - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Leah Murray Albert - Public Arbitrator
Theresa Joan Rosenberg - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
Diane Ciccone 03/20/2026
